description_long: This church is a grand, historic stone structure with a red-tiled gabled roof and two prominent bell towers at the front, each topped with domed spires. The facade features ornate stonework, arched windows, and three main entry doors beneath a decorative rose window. Along the sides, tall, narrow windows are evenly spaced, and the building is surrounded by low landscaping. The rear of the church includes a semi-circular apse and additional smaller structures, likely chapels or vestries, with their own red-tiled roofs. Architectural details such as pilasters, cornices, and decorative reliefs are visible throughout the exterior. The overall impression is of a stately, traditional European church, likely from the Baroque or late Renaissance period.
description: A large, ornate stone church with twin bell towers, red-tiled roof, arched windows, and detailed stonework, exuding historic European architectural charm.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long nave, tall arched windows, and landscaped base, with one bell tower and the main roof visible.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the twin bell towers, three main entry doors, rose window, and richly decorated facade.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the nave, arched windows, and both bell towers visible, but less landscaping detail.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the semi-circular apse, smaller attached structures, and the full length of the red-tiled roof.
